title: Sentinel Outbound Event
description: Events Sentinel emits to Anoryx-AI-Orchestrator (and onward to Delta) over the Redis Streams bus. Every variant carries the four stable IDs from contracts/ids.md (LOCKED, IMMUTABLE) plus event_id, event_timestamp, and request_id. These IDs are the join key between Sentinel events and Delta records. NORMATIVE DISPATCH: variant selection is determined SOLELY by oneOf plus the event_type const on each variant; the const values are unique and the variants are mutually exclusive, so exactly one matches a valid record. VALIDATOR DIALECT: Sentinel, Anoryx-AI-Orchestrator, and Delta MUST all validate these records with a JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 validator so no parser-differential exists. All objects are closed (additionalProperties:false) and all fields are bounded (maxLength/maximum/maxItems) per the F-001 audit findings: closed schemas remove a silent smuggling channel and bounded fields remove a DoS-via-inspection vector. Attribution carried on events is always the server-resolved value (see contracts/openapi.yaml virtual-API-key key-to-ID binding and id_context_mismatch), never a raw client-supplied header.
$comment: Dispatch is normative via oneOf + the event_type const on each variant under JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. The 'discriminator' keyword below is a NON-NORMATIVE hint for OpenAPI tooling ONLY; it is not a 2020-12 keyword and has NO validation effect under this dialect. Do not rely on it for correctness.

tenant_id.description: Stable tenant identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. Server-resolved value, not a raw client header.

team_id.description: Stable team identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md.

project_id.description: Stable project identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md.

agent_id.description: Internal Sentinel component name (lowercase slug). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. NOT the end-user AI model name.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event (UUID v4). Deduplication key on the bus.

event_timestamp.description: Event emission time (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC).

request_id.description: Correlation ID matching the originating gateway request (X-Request-Id in contracts/openapi.yaml). Links the event back to the request that produced it. Charset is restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

UsageEvent.description: Per-request token/cost/latency usage record. Drives the Delta client-side cost estimate; cost_estimate_cents is an estimate, not an authoritative bill.

model.description: End-user AI model name reported by the gateway (e.g. the value sent in the request body).

cost_estimate_cents.description: Client-side cost estimate in cents. Estimate only; not an authoritative bill.

PiiBlockedEvent.description: Emitted when the data-protection engine detects and acts on PII.

pattern_name.description: Name of the PII detection pattern that matched (e.g. 'us_ssn', 'email'). Never the matched value.

severity.description: Severity of the detected PII exposure.

sample_excerpt_redacted.description: OPTIONAL already-redacted excerpt for audit context. This field is intended to be ALREADY REDACTED by the data-protection engine before emission; it MUST NEVER contain raw PII or the matched value. The pattern is a MARKER-PRESENCE check ONLY: it requires a redaction marker ('[REDACTED]' or '***') somewhere in the value. It does NOT guarantee the ABSENCE of raw PII and provides NO structural prevention of raw PII riding alongside a marker. Full PII suppression is the emitter's runtime obligation, not a schema guarantee. Consumers MUST treat this field as potentially-unsafe display data requiring consumer-side validation before rendering or storage. Emitters that cannot guarantee redaction MUST omit this field.

InjectionDetectedEvent.description: Emitted when the defense engine flags a prompt-injection attempt.

rule_matched.description: Identifier of the detection rule that matched. Never the offending input text.

SecretLeakedEvent.description: Emitted when the defense engine detects a secret in traffic.

secret_type.description: Classification of the detected secret. The secret value itself is NEVER emitted.

direction.description: Traffic direction in which the secret was detected (inbound to model vs outbound to client).

PolicyViolatedEvent.description: Emitted when a Delta-sourced policy (contracts/policy.schema.json) is violated at the gateway.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the violated policy (UUID v4); joins to contracts/policy.schema.json policy_id.

violation_type.description: Classification of the violation (e.g. 'budget_exceeded', 'model_denied'). Charset is restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

ComplianceCheckedEvent.description: Emitted when a compliance control is evaluated for audit-ready evidence. 'status' reports the result of an individual control check; it does NOT assert certification or overall compliance.

framework.description: Audit framework the control belongs to.

control_id.description: Framework-specific control identifier. Charset is restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

status.description: Result of this individual control check. Audit-ready evidence, not a certification claim.

ShadowAiDetectedEvent.description: Emitted when traffic to an unsanctioned (shadow) AI endpoint is observed.

detected_endpoint.description: PII-SAFETY: host (and optionally path) of the detected shadow AI endpoint ONLY. Emitters MUST NOT log full URLs: query strings and fragments may carry API keys, tokens, or PII. Strip query params, fragments, and userinfo before emission. Structurally enforced: the value MUST NOT contain '?', '#', '@', or whitespace, so query strings, fragments, and userinfo cannot ride along. Bounded to 256 chars to cap inspection cost.

traffic_volume.description: Observed request count to the shadow endpoint over the detection window.

first_seen_at.description: When traffic to this endpoint was first observed (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC).
